LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. RULING: AT THE REQUEST OF COUNSEL, DUE TO THE UNAVAILABILITY OF DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL, COURT MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND CONTINUES THE MATTER TO MAY 9, 2008 FOR ORAL ARGUMENT: THE TENTATIVE RULING IS TO SUSTAIN THE DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-C0MPLAINANT DOES NOT OPPOSE THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. THE THIRD THROUGH SIXTH CAUSES ARE BARRED BY FAILURE TO FILE A GOVERNMENT CLAIM. IN THE THIRD THROUGH FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION, CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS SEEKING AFFIRMATIVE RELIEF BASED ON EVENTS THAT ARE SEPARATE AND DISTINCT FROM THE AUTO ACCIDENT. THE CLAIMS ALSO BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. IN THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS NOT AND CANNOT ALLEGE NEGLIGENCE ON HIS PART IN HIS DEALINGS WITH THE CROSS-DEFENDANTS. THERE IS NO BASIS FOR AN EQUITABLE INDEMNITY CLAIM. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-C0MPLAINANT DOES NOT OPPOSE THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. THE THIRD THROUGH SIXTH CAUSES ARE BARRED BY FAILURE TO FILE A GOVERNMENT CLAIM. IN THE THIRD THROUGH FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION, CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS SEEKING AFFIRMATIVE RELIEF BASED ON EVENTS THAT ARE SEPARATE AND DISTINCT FROM THE AUTO ACCIDENT. THE CLAIMS ALSO BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. IN THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS NOT AND CANNOT ALLEGE NEGLIGENCE ON HIS PART IN HIS DEALINGS WITH THE CROSS-DEFENDANTS. THERE IS NO BASIS FOR AN EQUITABLE INDEMNITY CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-09-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: WARREN METLITZKY, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3916) APPEARING FOR CROSS-DEFENDANT. GAIL J. LAMCHICK, ESQUIRE (LAW OFFICES OF BORIS E. EFRON, 650.851.8880) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-C0MPLAINANT DOES NOT OPPOSE THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. THE THIRD THROUGH SIXTH CAUSES ARE BARRED BY FAILURE TO FILE A GOVERNMENT CLAIM. IN THE THIRD THROUGH FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION, CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS SEEKING AFFIRMATIVE RELIEF BASED ON EVENTS THAT ARE SEPARATE AND DISTINCT FROM THE AUTO ACCIDENT. THE CLAIMS ALSO BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. IN THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS NOT AND CANNOT ALLEGE NEGLIGENCE ON HIS PART IN HIS DEALINGS WITH THE CROSS-DEFENDANTS. THERE IS NO BASIS FOR AN EQUITABLE INDEMNITY CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
